title: NATO Internship 2026: Paid 6-Month Opportunity at the NATO Liaison Office in Moldova
summary: The NATO Liaison Office in the Republic of Moldova has officially opened applications for its 2026 paid internship programme. This prestigious opportunity offers six internship positions for young professionals seeking hands-on experience in international relations, public diplomacy, and security cooperation. Based in Chisinau , the internship provides direct exposure to NATO’s diplomatic work and its cooperation with Moldova, making it an excellent stepping stone for careers in international organizations, diplomacy, and policy. Internship Overview The NATO Liaison Office (NLO) represents NATO in Moldova and plays a key role in facilitating political dialogue, supporting reforms, and promoting cooperation between NATO and Moldovan institutions. Key Details Location: Chisinau, Republic of Moldova Duration: 6 months (full-time) Working Hours: 38 hours per week Salary: 350 EUR/month Application Deadline: April 8, 2026 Available Internship Areas The programme offers six positions across three main areas: Defence Capacity Building (DCB) Interns will support project development and implementation related to strengthening defense capabilities and institutional reforms. Professional Development Programme (PDP) This role involves assisting in organizing, coordinating, and evaluating training programmes and capacity-building activities. Public Diplomacy (PD) Interns will contribute to communication strategies, public outreach, media monitoring, and social media content creation. Applicants can apply for multiple areas through a single application by indicating their preferences in the motivation letter. Key Responsibilities Selected interns will contribute to a variety of tasks, including: Supporting event organization such as seminars, trainings, and workshops Assisting with programme coordination and data analysis Drafting correspondence and reports in English and Romanian Preparing briefing notes and informational materials Monitoring media and producing news summaries (for PD roles) Supporting social media and communication campaigns (for PD roles) Benefits of the Internship The NATO internship offers a valuable professional development experience, including: Exposure to NATO’s operations and international cooperation frameworks Practical experience in diplomacy, project management, and communications Participation in high-level events and training activities Development of analytical and organizational skills Opportunity to improve English language proficiency Experience working in a multicultural, professional environment Eligibility Criteria Essential Requirements Candidates must: Be citizens of the Republic of Moldova Be at least 21 years old Be currently enrolled in or recently graduated (within one year) from relevant fields such as international relations, political science, or law Have proficiency in Romanian and fluency in English Possess knowledge of political and security issues Be proficient in Microsoft Office tools Demonstrate understanding of NATO–Moldova relations Be able to pass security screening Desirable Qualifications Experience in project management Background in public affairs or social media (especially for PD roles) Experience in event planning Required Skills Strong organizational and analytical abilities Excellent communication and writing skills Adaptability and ability to work under pressure Teamwork and initiative Recruitment Process The application process includes two main steps: Step 1: Application Submission Candidates must submit the following documents via email: CV Motivation letter (maximum 300 words, in English) Two reference letters Subject line format: Position – SURNAME NAME Step 2: Interview Shortlisted candidates (up to 25) will be invited for interviews. Working Conditions and Terms Full-time internship (6 months) Includes a one-month probation period Monthly stipend of 350 EUR No guarantee of employment after completion Diversity and Inclusion NATO is committed to promoting diversity and inclusion in its workforce. Applications are encouraged from all qualified candidates regardless of gender, ethnicity, religion, disability, or background. Final Thoughts This internship at the NATO Liaison Office in Moldova offers a unique opportunity to gain first-hand experience in international diplomacy, security cooperation, and public policy. For young professionals interested in global affairs and defense policy, this programme provides a strong foundation for a future career in international organizations. For more information click here Discover more opportunities here The post NATO Internship 2026: Paid 6-Month Opportunity at the NATO Liaison Office in Moldova appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Fully Funded Tufts One Health Fellowship Program 2026: A Global Health Leadership Opportunity in the United States
summary: Applications are now open for the Tufts One Health Fellowship Program 2026, a prestigious and fully funded global health leadership initiative hosted by Tufts University in collaboration with leading international partners. This highly competitive fellowship is designed to equip emerging professionals with the expertise, practical experience, and global networks required to tackle complex health challenges using a multidisciplinary approach. The program is built on the “One Health” concept, which recognizes the interconnectedness of human, animal, and environmental health. By integrating these sectors, fellows are trained to develop sustainable and innovative solutions to pressing global health issues. About the Tufts One Health Fellowship The Tufts One Health Fellowship is an intensive program that blends academic training, fieldwork, and professional development. It is specifically tailored for early- to mid-career professionals who are committed to improving public health systems in their home countries. Through a combination of coursework, mentorship, and real-world exposure, fellows gain the tools necessary to address health challenges that cross traditional disciplinary boundaries. Program Structure and Experience Participants in the fellowship benefit from a comprehensive learning journey that includes: Academic and Leadership Training Fellows receive advanced instruction in global health leadership, interdisciplinary collaboration, and systems thinking. The curriculum is designed to strengthen their ability to analyze and respond to complex health issues from multiple perspectives. Field Placement in the United States A key component of the program is a professional placement within a relevant organization in the United States. This hands-on experience allows fellows to work directly on real-world public health challenges, gaining practical skills and insights into international health systems. Mentorship and Networking Participants are paired with experienced global health professionals who provide guidance and mentorship throughout the program. Fellows also join a diverse international network of peers and alumni, fostering long-term collaboration and knowledge exchange. Benefits of the Fellowship The Tufts One Health Fellowship is fully funded, ensuring that selected participants can focus entirely on learning and professional growth. Benefits include: Full financial coverage of program participation costs Access to high-level global health training Placement opportunities in leading organizations in the United States Mentorship from experienced practitioners Networking opportunities with international experts and alumni Exposure to interdisciplinary approaches in global health This funding structure removes financial barriers and enables fellows to fully engage in the program experience. Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet the following requirements: Be early- to mid-career professionals with a strong interest in public health Typically be between 25 and 40 years old (may vary slightly) Hold an academic or professional background in relevant fields such as: Medicine Veterinary science Environmental science Public health Social sciences Policy or related disciplines Have at least two years of relevant professional experience or active engagement in health-related work Demonstrate strong English communication skills Candidates should also show a clear commitment to improving health outcomes in their communities. Eligible Countries The Tufts One Health Fellowship is currently open to applicants from the following countries: Kenya Ethiopia Uganda Rwanda Applicants must be citizens or current residents of one of these countries. Eligibility may vary depending on the specific cohort, so candidates are encouraged to consult the official website for updates. Final Thoughts The Tufts One Health Fellowship Program 2026 represents a unique opportunity for emerging leaders in global health to gain world-class training, international exposure, and practical experience. By focusing on the intersection of human, animal, and environmental health, the program prepares fellows to become innovative problem-solvers capable of addressing some of the world’s most pressing health challenges. For professionals seeking to expand their impact and contribute to sustainable health solutions, this fellowship offers a powerful platform for growth and leadership. For more information click here Discover more opportunities here The post Fully Funded Tufts One Health Fellowship Program 2026: A Global Health Leadership Opportunity in the United States appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: UN Internships 2026: HR, Project Management, Data Analytics & ICT Opportunities with UNHCR, UNOPS & International Organization for Migration
summary: If you are a student or recent graduate aiming to build a global career within the United Nations system, multiple internship opportunities are now open across Europe and beyond. These positions offer hands-on experience in human resources, infrastructure and project management, procurement data analytics, and information technology within leading UN agencies. With placements in Poland, Denmark, and Panama, these internships provide a unique opportunity to work in international, multicultural environments while contributing to impactful global missions. Internship Overview Several UN agencies are offering internships across diverse fields: Human Resources Internship – Warsaw, Poland (UNHCR) Infrastructure Project Management Internship – Copenhagen, Denmark (UNOPS) Procurement Data Analytics Internship – Copenhagen, Denmark (UNOPS) ICT Internship – Panama City (IOM) Deadline for Applications UNHCR HR Internship: April 6, 2026 UNOPS Internships: April 6–7, 2026 IOM ICT Internship: Check link for details Early applications are strongly recommended due to high competition. 1. Human Resources Internship – UNHCR (Warsaw, Poland) About the Organization UNHCR is a global organization established in 1950, working in over 130 countries to protect refugees, internally displaced persons, and stateless populations. The organization plays a critical role in humanitarian response and international protection. Internship Details Location: Warsaw, Poland Duration: 4 months (extendable) Type: Full-time Start Date: As soon as possible Key Responsibilities The HR intern will support core human resources functions, including: Maintaining personnel records and ensuring confidentiality Supporting absence management systems Assisting in recruitment processes and shortlisting Supporting onboarding and training delivery Performing administrative and reporting tasks Learning Outcomes Interns will gain: Practical experience in HR operations Exposure to international recruitment processes Understanding of HR compliance and personnel management Experience working in a multi-country office context APPLY HERE 2. Infrastructure Project Management Internship – UNOPS (Copenhagen) About the Role This internship is part of the Infrastructure and Project Management Group, which focuses on delivering sustainable infrastructure and strengthening project management systems globally. Key Responsibilities Supporting design review processes and coordination Tracking infrastructure project documentation Assisting in risk and coordination meetings Conducting data analysis and research Supporting compliance with UNOPS standards Learning Outcomes Exposure to international infrastructure standards Understanding project lifecycle management Experience with global project coordination Skills in research, reporting, and data analysis APPLY HERE 3. Procurement Data Analytics Internship – UNOPS (Copenhagen) Role Overview This internship focuses on transforming procurement data into actionable insights using analytics, dashboards, and digital tools. Key Responsibilities Data collection, cleaning, and validation Supporting procurement analytics and reporting Developing dashboards and visualization tools Exploring AI-enabled data processes Conducting research on procurement trends Learning Outcomes Hands-on experience in data analytics and visualization Exposure to AI tools in organizational processes Development of technical and analytical skills Understanding procurement’s role in sustainable development APPLY HERE 4. ICT Internship – IOM (Panama City) About the Opportunity The ICT Internship with IOM focuses on supporting information and communication technologies within migration-related operations. Key Highlights Location: Panama City Includes stipend Focus on IT systems, digital tools, and communication infrastructure This is an excellent opportunity for candidates interested in technology and humanitarian operations. APPLY HERE Benefits of These Internships Across all positions, selected candidates can expect: Practical experience within UN agencies Exposure to international and multicultural teams Professional skill development in specialized fields Networking opportunities with global experts Financial support (allowances or stipends depending on the role) Travel reimbursement in some cases Eligibility Criteria While requirements vary slightly by position, general eligibility includes: Current students or recent graduates (within 2–3 years) Minimum of two years of undergraduate study completed Strong proficiency in English (minimum B2 level) Academic background relevant to the internship field Strong organizational, communication, and analytical skills Working Conditions Full-time roles (typically 40 hours per week) On-site internships in respective duty stations Interns must handle visa, health insurance, and administrative requirements Diversity and Inclusion All UN agencies strongly encourage applications from diverse backgrounds. Opportunities are open regardless of race, gender, nationality, disability, or identity. Candidates with refugee or stateless backgrounds are especially encouraged to apply. Final Thoughts These internships represent a gateway into the United Nations system, offering invaluable experience in global development, humanitarian work, and international administration. Whether your interest lies in human resources, infrastructure, data analytics, or ICT, these roles provide a solid foundation for a future international career. Discover more opportunities here The post UN Internships 2026: HR, Project Management, Data Analytics ICT Opportunities with UNHCR, UNOPS International Organization for Migration appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Internet Society Mid Career Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Global Leadership Programme on Digital Transformation and Internet Governance
summary: The Internet Society Mid Career Fellowship 2026 is a prestigious, fully funded global leadership programme designed for experienced professionals working at the intersection of technology, policy, and society. This seven-month fellowship equips mid-career leaders with the knowledge, tools, and strategic insights needed to navigate complex digital challenges and shape the future of the Internet. In today’s interconnected world, challenges such as climate change, global pandemics, economic disruption, and digital inequality are deeply intertwined. This fellowship responds to that reality by preparing leaders to operate effectively in environments characterized by volatility, uncertainty, complexity, and ambiguity (VUCA). The programme is highly competitive and selects a cohort of 15 fellows globally, offering them access to world-class institutions, expert faculty, and a transformative learning experience. About the Internet Society Mid Career Fellowship The Mid Career Fellowship is designed for professionals who are already established in their careers and are working in roles that influence or are impacted by the Internet ecosystem. The programme focuses on strengthening leadership capacity while deepening participants’ understanding of digital systems and global Internet governance. Programme Vision The fellowship aims to: Empower leaders to address global digital challenges Promote inclusive, innovative, and strategic leadership Strengthen global collaboration across sectors Ensure the Internet remains open, secure, and globally connected Programme Structure and Learning Model The fellowship runs for seven months, from June to December 2026, and combines structured learning, collaboration, and practical application. Key Features of the Programme Cohort size: 15 fellows Duration: 7 months Weekly commitment: Approximately 6 hours Mode: Primarily virtual with global collaboration Core Programme Components The fellowship is structured into five key modules, each addressing critical aspects of the digital ecosystem. 1. Internet Critical Properties and Enablers Understanding the foundational architecture of the Internet Exploring infrastructure, protocols, and governance 2. Digital Transformation and Key Internet Issues Examining emerging digital challenges Addressing issues such as access, inclusion, and cybersecurity 3. Digital Futures and Strategic Foresight Anticipating future trends and disruptions Developing forward-thinking strategies 4. Systems and Design Thinking Applying systems thinking to complex challenges Designing innovative and sustainable solutions 5. Leadership and Decision-Making Strengthening leadership skills Navigating uncertainty and complexity Additional Learning Elements Participants will also engage in: Live lectures and expert-led sessions Case studies and practical exercises Seminars on digital geopolitics and advocacy Cross-sector collaboration and networking Final symposium to present their work Fellowship Benefits The Mid Career Fellowship offers a wide range of professional and personal development benefits. Key Benefits Access to world-class educational institutions and experts Advanced knowledge in digital transformation and Internet governance Leadership training tailored to global challenges Global networking opportunities with diverse professionals Practical experience through applied learning and case studies Opportunity to showcase work at an international symposium Eligibility Criteria The fellowship targets experienced professionals actively working within the Internet ecosystem. Who Can Apply Applicants must: Be mid-career professionals in leadership roles Have at least 10 years of relevant experience Work in areas related to: Technology Policy Economics Social impact Be involved in addressing digital challenges or transformation Additional Requirements Applicants must also: Demonstrate interest in solving global challenges Be willing to collaborate in multidisciplinary teams Have reliable internet access and necessary devices Commit approximately 6 hours per week for 7 months Be proficient in English (written and spoken) Preferred Applicants The programme strongly encourages applications from: Least Developed Countries (LDCs) Small Island Developing States (SIDS) Asia-Pacific region Why You Should Apply This fellowship is a transformative opportunity for professionals seeking to: Strengthen leadership in digital and Internet governance Gain global exposure and collaboration experience Develop strategic thinking and foresight skills Address complex global challenges Advance their career in technology, policy, or development Application Process Applicants must complete an Online Application Form. Selection Process The selection process includes: Application submission Multi-step evaluation by a selection committee Consideration of: Professional background Leadership potential Geographic and gender diversity Shortlisted candidates are selected based on merit and diversity considerations. Key Dates and Timeline Application Opens: Week of March 30, 2026 Application Deadline: April 20, 2026 Selection Period: April – May 2026 Programme Start: Early June 2026 Programme End: December 2026 (Final Symposium) Applicants are encouraged to apply early to ensure full consideration. Learn more about the Internet Society Mid Career Fellowship 2026 HERE. Discover more global Fellowship opportunities on OFY. title: Apply Now: Doha Debates Town Hall 2026 in Toronto (Fully Funded Youth Debate Opportunity)
summary: Young people passionate about global conversations, sports culture, and critical thinking now have an exciting opportunity to join a Doha Debates Town Hall in Toronto, Canada. Scheduled for July 2026, this unique program brings together a diverse youth cast to explore one of the most pressing questions in modern sports: how big money is reshaping football fandom . This article provides a detailed overview of the program, including the debate theme, participation requirements, benefits, and how to apply. About the Doha Debates Town Hall Program The Doha Debates Town Hall is an interactive global platform that engages young people in meaningful discussions on complex global issues. Participants are selected to join a “youth cast” and take part in guided workshops, discussions, and a professionally produced town hall event. The Toronto edition builds on previous successful town halls, including one hosted in Buenos Aires, and aims to amplify youth voices on issues that intersect culture, economics, and identity. Debate Topic: Big Money and the Future of Football Fandom The 2026 town hall focuses on a timely and provocative topic: What does football fandom mean when big money is involved? Two decades ago, football clubs were often closely tied to local identity and community roots. Today, the landscape has shifted dramatically. Clubs like Manchester United are publicly traded and boast global fanbases numbering in the hundreds of millions, many of whom live far from the club’s home country. Despite this distance, emotional attachment remains strong. Fans celebrate victories, mourn defeats, and fiercely defend their teams online and offline. This raises important questions about identity and belonging in a globalized sports economy. Key Issues to Be Explored: The influence of private equity firms and sovereign wealth funds in football The rise of multi-club ownership models The globalization of fan communities The evolving meaning of loyalty and identity in sports Proponents argue that increased investment improves the quality and reach of the game. Critics, however, question how commercialization impacts authenticity and fan connection. The town hall will explore these perspectives through open dialogue and debate. Why You Should Apply Participating in the Doha Debates Town Hall offers: A platform to share your voice on a global stage Exposure to diverse perspectives on a major global issue Opportunities to build critical thinking and communication skills A chance to engage with a community of passionate, like-minded youth Participation Requirements To be considered for the program, applicants must meet the following requirements: Application Deadline Submit your application by April 16, 2026 Pre-Event Commitments Attend four virtual workshops (60–90 minutes each) between June 1 and June 26, 2026 Sessions will be scheduled after: 7:00 p.m. AST 4:00 p.m. EST Complete approximately 5 hours of required reading during the same period Availability for In-Person Program For participants based in Doha: Must be in Toronto from July 3 to July 10, 2026 Must have no conflicting commitments (including remote work or classes) For participants based in Toronto: Must be available from July 5 to July 8, 2026 Daily participation required between 9:00 a.m. and 9:00 p.m. Travel, Stipend, and Additional Considerations Travel and Accommodation Fully covered for participants traveling from Doha For participants already in Canada, support is provided on a case-by-case basis Stipend Selected participants will receive approximately: 375 CAD (or 1,000 QAR ) Media and Broadcasting The town hall will be professionally filmed Content will be published on Doha Debates platforms and may be broadcast more widely Important Notes for Applicants Doha Debates emphasizes curiosity, authenticity, and critical thinking. Applicants are encouraged to: Engage thoughtfully with the topic Demonstrate originality in their responses Use artificial intelligence tools responsibly and ethically when completing their application How to Apply Interested candidates should prepare their application materials and submit them before the deadline. Ensure you are able to meet all participation requirements before applying. Click here, to Register. This is more than just a discussion—it is an opportunity to examine how global forces are reshaping one of the world’s most beloved sports, and what that means for fans everywhere. If you are ready to contribute to this conversation, consider applying before the April 16 deadline. Discover more similar opportunities here. The post Apply Now: Doha Debates Town Hall 2026 in Toronto (Fully Funded Youth Debate Opportunity) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Apply for the Teaching with Primary Sources (TPS) Internship 2026 | Paid Library of Congress Internship
summary: The Teaching with Primary Sources (TPS) Internship Program offers a unique opportunity for students and recent graduates to gain hands-on experience in education, history, library science, and museum studies while working with one of the most prestigious knowledge institutions in the world. Hosted by the Office of the Librarian, this program provides both remote and onsite internship opportunities designed to support the educational use of primary sources. Program Overview The Teaching with Primary Sources (TPS) program is administered by the Professional Learning and Outreach Initiatives Office at the Library of Congress . It focuses on enhancing education through the use of digitized primary sources, offering teaching materials, professional development, and partnerships that support educators and learners. At the heart of the TPS initiative is the TPS Consortium—a nationwide network of partner organizations that develop educational programming and tools using the Library’s vast digital collections. This work aligns with the Library’s broader mission to engage, inspire, and inform the public through access to knowledge and creativity. The TPS Internship Program allows interns to actively contribute to this mission by supporting the development of educational materials and programming for K–12 audiences, educators, and families. Internship Structure and Opportunities Each semester, the program selects up to two interns: One remote intern One onsite intern based in Washington, DC Program Duration Short-term internships offered during Fall and Spring semesters Work Schedule Onsite Intern : Up to 35 hours per week (9:00 AM – 4:30 PM), five days a week, including Saturday Remote Intern : 20 hours per week (Monday–Friday), with flexible scheduling, but must overlap with 9:30 AM – 3:00 PM Eastern Time Compensation and Benefits The program offers competitive stipends depending on the internship format: Remote Internship $500 monthly stipend (20 hours/week) Onsite Internship $750 monthly stipend (35 hours/week) $255/month for local transportation Up to $1,250/month for housing support Travel allowance of up to $1,000 each way (for interns relocating to/from Washington, DC) Additional Benefits Access to Library of Congress internal discounts Academic Credit While the Library of Congress does not directly provide academic credit, interns may coordinate with their academic institutions in advance to receive credit for their participation. Why Apply? The TPS Internship Program is an excellent opportunity for aspiring educators, researchers, and cultural professionals to: Gain practical experience with primary source-based education Collaborate with experts and national education networks Contribute to meaningful educational content used across the United States Build a strong foundation for careers in education, libraries, museums, and public history Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet the following requirements: Be a U.S. citizen or permanent resident Be at least 18 years old Be an undergraduate or graduate student in good standing , or a recent graduate (within six months) Demonstrate interest in: Education Library science History Museum studies Have experience (academic, volunteer, or professional) working with learners aged 9+, families, or K–12 educators Show strong collaboration and communication skills Key Responsibilities TPS interns play an active role in developing and supporting educational initiatives. Responsibilities include: Identifying primary source materials and effective learning strategies for K–12 audiences Learning about the Library’s collections, programs, and educational mission Assisting in planning and implementing educational programming Conducting research and consulting with Library experts Designing and executing a special project Contributing insights to the TPS Teachers Network Onsite Intern (Additional Responsibilities) Engage directly with young visitors (ages 9+) and families Develop and deliver in-person educational activities based on primary sources Application Process How to Apply Submit all required materials via email to: tpscolorado@msudenver.edu Subject line format: “TPS Internship – [Applicant’s Full Name]” Required Documents Resume Statement of purpose (250 words) outlining academic and career goals Project proposal (max 100 words) using digitized primary sources from the Library of Congress Transcript (unofficial accepted) Two academic or professional references (contact details only) Indication of availability (remote or onsite) Application Deadlines and Internship Dates Fall 2026 Application Period: March 2, 2026 – May 11, 2026 Internship Duration: Mid-September – Mid-December 2026 Important: All materials must be submitted by 11:59 PM (local time) on the deadline date. Late applications will not be considered. Note: The program does not provide feedback on individual applications, but general inquiries are welcome. The Teaching with Primary Sources Internship Program stands out as a highly enriching experience for individuals passionate about education and history. With both remote and onsite options, structured mentorship, and access to world-class resources, this internship offers a valuable pathway into impactful careers in education and cultural institutions. If you meet the eligibility criteria and are eager to work with primary sources to inspire learners, this program is well worth applying for. Discover more similar opportunities here The post Apply for the Teaching with Primary Sources (TPS) Internship 2026 | Paid Library of Congress Internship appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Chatham House Academy Fellowship 2026/2027 (Richard and Susan Hayden Fellowship): Fully Funded 10-Month Programme in London for Global Leaders in International Affairs
summary: The Chatham House Academy Fellowship 2026/2027, also known as the Richard and Susan Hayden Academy Fellowship, is a prestigious, fully funded international fellowship designed for early- to mid-career professionals from around the world. Hosted by Chatham House, one of the world’s leading policy institutes, this programme offers a unique opportunity to conduct independent research, develop leadership skills, and engage with global experts on pressing international issues. This fellowship is ideal for professionals working in academia, government, NGOs, business, civil society, or media who are committed to addressing global challenges and contributing to evidence-based policymaking. About Chatham House and the Academy Fellowship Chatham House, also known as The Royal Institute of International Affairs, is a globally recognized think tank dedicated to analyzing and addressing international challenges. Through its Academy, the institution develops future leaders by combining research, leadership training, and global networking. The Richard and Susan Hayden Academy Fellowship is a flagship programme that supports individuals in conducting independent research while actively contributing to Chatham House’s mission of building a secure, sustainable, and just world. Fellowship Overview The fellowship provides a structured and immersive experience combining research, leadership development, and institutional engagement. Key Details Programme Duration: 10 months Fellowship Period: October 2026 – July 2027 Location: Chatham House, London (with hybrid working options) Deadline: April 17, 2026 Eligibility: Open to applicants from all nationalities Fellows are expected to commit full-time to the programme and cannot undertake other work or studies simultaneously. Fellowship Structure The fellowship is divided into three main components, ensuring a balanced and impactful experience. 1. Independent Research Project (60%) Fellows design and conduct their own research project aligned with their expertise and interests. Key aspects include: Selection of a research topic relevant to international affairs Guidance from a Chatham House expert Development of policy-relevant outputs 2. Contribution to Chatham House Research (20%) Fellows actively participate in ongoing research initiatives within the institute. Activities include: Collaborating with research teams Supporting institutional research priorities Engaging in policy discussions and outputs 3. Leadership Masterclass Programme (20%) This core component focuses on building leadership capacity and professional development. Leadership Programme Components The leadership programme is designed to equip fellows with skills needed for global leadership. Key Elements Induction Week Five-day orientation programme Introduction to fellowship structure and expectations Thematic Sessions Discussions on global challenges Interaction with subject matter experts Leadership Skills Workshops Policy impact strategies Stakeholder engagement Crisis management Negotiation techniques Leadership Briefings Engagement with global leaders Insights into leadership strategies and experiences Project Presentations Fellows present research progress Peer review and feedback Personal Development Coaching One-on-one coaching sessions Goal setting and professional growth Media Training Interview skills for television and radio Mock interviews and feedback Career Mentoring (Optional) Mentorship opportunities Career guidance and development Financial Benefits and Support The fellowship provides comprehensive financial support to ensure participants can fully focus on their work. Financial Package Monthly stipend: £2,365 (to cover living expenses in London) Coverage of relocation costs: Flights and travel Temporary accommodation Visa-related expenses Payment of UK Immigration Healthcare Surcharge Funding for research activities: Fieldwork Conferences Publications Events Additional Benefits Access to Chatham House’s global network Participation in high-level policy discussions Opportunities to host research roundtables Five-year membership at Chatham House after completion Continued access to alumni and faculty networks Eligibility Criteria Who Can Apply Applicants from all countries (no nationality restrictions) Early- to mid-career professionals Required Qualifications Bachelor’s degree or equivalent Experience conducting research Professional background in: Academia NGOs Government Business Civil society Media Strong interest in international affairs Additional Requirements Demonstrated leadership potential Commitment to Chatham House’s mission Ability to communicate effectively in English Why You Should Apply This fellowship offers a rare opportunity to: Conduct independent research at a leading global think tank Develop leadership and policy skills Engage with international experts and policymakers Expand your professional network Contribute to solving global challenges Application Requirements Applicants must submit: CV or résumé Names of two referees Research proposal aligned with fellowship themes Selection Criteria Candidates are evaluated based on: Research experience and academic background Leadership potential Relevance of proposed research Motivation and commitment Ability to create positive impact Selection Process Stage 1 Application review and shortlisting Stage 2 Interview via Zoom with senior management Successful candidates will be notified via email. Learn more about the The Chatham House Academy Fellowship HERE. Application Deadline The deadline to apply for the Chatham House Academy Fellowship 2026/2027 is: April 17, 2026 Applicants are encouraged to submit early to ensure a complete and competitive application. See our Careers page for full details. Discover more global Fellowship opportunities HERE. The post Chatham House Academy Fellowship 2026/2027 (Richard and Susan Hayden Fellowship): Fully Funded 10-Month Programme in London for Global Leaders in International Affairs appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: UNFPA Remote Internships 2026 (People Strategy Roles): Complete Guide to People Analytics, HR Policy & Leadership Development Opportunities
summary: The United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA) is offering highly competitive remote internship opportunities for 2026 within its Division for Human Resources (DHR). These internships are part of UNFPA’s evolving People Strategy and align with its 2026–2029 Strategic Plan, focusing on digital transformation, workforce planning, and organizational development. This comprehensive internship programme includes three specialized tracks: People Analytics AI, Human Resources Policy, and Learning Leadership Development. In addition to gaining practical experience, applicants must complete a structured application process through the official UNFPA People Strategy Internship Application Form. This article provides a complete, detailed breakdown of the internship roles, eligibility, responsibilities, application process, and additional requirements. About UNFPA and Its Global Mission UNFPA is the United Nations agency dedicated to ensuring reproductive health, gender equality, and population data systems worldwide. Its mission centers on three transformative goals: Ending preventable maternal deaths Ending unmet need for family planning Ending gender-based violence and harmful practices Through its strategic framework, UNFPA supports countries in achieving sustainable development goals and adapting to demographic changes using evidence-based policies. Overview of the UNFPA 2026 People Strategy Internships UNFPA’s Division for Human Resources is recruiting interns across three strategic functional areas. Available Internship Tracks People Analytics AI Intern Human Resources Policy Intern Learning Leadership Development Intern General Internship Details Location: Remote (with optional in-person placement in New York or Nairobi if locally recruited) Duration: 6 months Schedule: Full-time Timeline: Expected between April and December 2026 Organizational Unit: Division for Human Resources (DHR) Interns will work under structured supervision, with defined work plans, continuous feedback, and performance evaluation. Internship Track 1: People Analytics AI Intern This role is focused on transforming HR through data, automation, and artificial intelligence. Key Responsibilities Collect, clean, and analyze workforce data across recruitment, performance, and mobility Maintain HR dashboards using Power BI Translate complex datasets into actionable insights for leadership Automate processes using AI tools such as Gemini Develop AI prompts for policy analysis and sentiment evaluation Monitor HR KPIs and troubleshoot data systems Prepare reports and presentations for diverse audiences Required Skills Experience with spreadsheets (Excel, Google Sheets) Familiarity with data visualization tools (Power BI, Tableau, Looker) Basic knowledge of SQL or Python (preferred) Experience with generative AI tools (ChatGPT, Gemini) Strong analytical and storytelling skills Learn more here Internship Track 2: Human Resources Policy Intern This role focuses on HR governance, policy design, and organizational systems. Key Responsibilities Support development and revision of HR policies and procedures Conduct research on HR trends and best practices across the UN system Analyze and recommend policy improvements Assist in policy implementation and communication Draft briefing notes, reports, and position papers Manage knowledge systems and internal documentation Collaborate with stakeholders across UN agencies Required Skills Strong analytical and writing skills Interest in HR policy and governance Research and documentation capabilities Ability to work collaboratively in multicultural teams Learn more here Internship Track 3: Learning Leadership Development Intern This role focuses on training, onboarding, and leadership capacity development. Key Responsibilities Support onboarding and leadership programmes Coordinate learning activities and track participation Develop training materials and toolkits Conduct research on talent development trends Support analytics and dashboard creation Use AI tools to enhance learning systems Prepare communication and reporting materials Required Skills Interest in learning and development Experience with digital tools and AI technologies Strong communication and analytical abilities Experience working in global or remote teams Learn more here Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet at least one of the following: Be enrolled in a postgraduate programme (Master’s or higher) Be in the final year of a bachelor’s degree Have graduated within the last 12 months Be enrolled in a postgraduate traineeship programme Additional Requirements Fluency in English Strong communication and teamwork skills Ability to work in multicultural environments Application Process: UNFPA People Strategy Internship Form Applicants must complete the official UNFPA application form and provide detailed information across multiple sections. Section 1: Personal Information Applicants must provide: Full name Email address Nationality Country of residence Work location (city/country) Availability (April–December 2026) Time zone Age Section 2: Eligibility Screening Applicants must confirm: Student status or recent graduation (within 12 months) Highest level of education (Bachelor, Master, PhD) Current or ongoing education English proficiency Experience in HR, learning, or talent management Experience with UN or international institutions (if any) Field of study Experience working with global or remote teams Required Application Materials Applicants must submit: CV Cover letter Additional supporting documents (optional) LinkedIn profile (optional) Video Requirement Applicants must upload a short video (maximum 2 minutes) answering: Introduction Motivation for applying to UNFPA HR Division Relevant experience and contributions Motivation Statement Applicants must provide a written statement explaining: Why they are applying What value they will bring to the team Application Deadline The deadline to apply for the UNFPA People Strategy Internships is: April 19, 2026 Applicants are strongly encouraged to apply early and ensure all documents are accessible. Financial Aspects UNFPA internships are primarily learning opportunities. Key Financial Details No salary is provided A stipend may be offered to cover basic expenses (if not externally funded) Interns must cover: Travel Accommodation Living expenses Additional Requirements Mandatory medical insurance Proof of insurance required before start Learning Outcomes and Benefits Interns will gain: Understanding of UNFPA HR systems and global operations Experience in HR analytics, policy, or leadership development Exposure to UN technical frameworks and processes Opportunities to develop professional communication skills Networking with global UN professionals Experience working in multicultural teams Why You Should Apply These internships provide a strong foundation for careers in: International development Human resources Data analytics Policy and governance United Nations and global organizations Participants will gain hands-on experience, build global networks, and contribute to meaningful international work. Click HERE to learn more and apply for the UNFPA Remote Internships 2026. Discover more other global Internship opportunities on OFY. title: Fully Funded EU4Ocean Summer School 2026 at the Mediterranean Sea and Black Sea
summary: If you are a student or early-career professional looking for a fully funded international opportunity in 2026, the EU4Ocean Summer School on Ocean Literacy Collaboration is one of the most prestigious global programs you should not miss. This opportunity offers participants the chance to travel, learn, network, and contribute to global ocean sustainability efforts—all in an immersive academic and professional environment. What is the EU4Ocean Summer School? The EU4Ocean Summer School is an international training program focused on ocean literacy, sustainability, and collaboration. It brings together a small, highly competitive group of participants from across the world to engage in interdisciplinary learning and problem-solving. Participants explore key topics such as: Ocean sustainability and climate change Nature-based solutions Policy and governance in ocean science Project design and implementation Communication, storytelling, and advocacy Stakeholder engagement and collaboration The program aims to build a new generation of leaders capable of driving change in ocean conservation and sustainable development. Program Benefits (Fully Funded Opportunity) One of the strongest advantages of this program is its funding structure. Selected participants typically receive: Free accommodation (shared rooms) Meals (lunches and dinners) Access to all training sessions and materials Networking opportunities with global experts Certificates of participation Limited travel grants for selected participants These benefits make it an accessible opportunity for individuals who may not otherwise afford international exposure. Who Can Apply? (Eligible Applicants) The EU4Ocean Summer School is open to a wide range of applicants globally. However, priority is often given to specific groups. Eligible Categories of People Students (Master’s and PhD levels) Early-career professionals Researchers Youth leaders and activists Professionals working in environment, climate, or sustainability Academic and Professional Requirements Applicants should: Be enrolled in or have completed a Master’s or PhD program, or Be in the early stages of their professional career Demonstrate a strong interest in ocean-related topics Show motivation for collaboration and global impact The program welcomes individuals from all disciplines, not just marine science, as long as they show a passion for ocean sustainability. Eligible Countries and Regions This opportunity is international, meaning applicants can come from: Europe (EU countries) Non-EU countries Global applicants from various regions However, some editions prioritize participants from: Mediterranean Sea region Black Sea region Despite this, applicants from Africa, Asia, and other continents are encouraged to apply, especially those with strong profiles and motivation. Key Skills and Learning Outcomes Participants in the EU4Ocean Summer School gain: Practical skills in project design and management Knowledge of global ocean initiatives Improved communication and storytelling skills Exposure to international collaboration frameworks Understanding of policy and governance systems The program is specifically designed to empower participants to lead and influence change in ocean-related sectors globally. How to Apply To apply for the EU4Ocean Summer School 2026: Access the official application form (your provided link) Fill in personal and academic details Provide motivation and background information Submit required supporting documents (if requested) Review and submit before the deadline Selection is highly competitive, so ensure your application clearly demonstrates: Passion for ocean sustainability Leadership potential Relevant experience or academic background Apply here Why You Should Apply This is not just a training—it is a career-transforming global opportunity. Here is why it stands out: Fully funded international exposure Direct interaction with global experts Opportunity to join a global network of ocean advocates Career advancement in sustainability and environmental sectors Contribution to global initiatives like the UN Ocean Decade Participants leave the program with enhanced knowledge, global connections, and practical tools to implement real-world solutions. Deadline 27th April 2026 The EU4Ocean Summer School 2026 is a highly prestigious, fully funded international training opportunity that empowers young leaders to shape the future of ocean sustainability. If you are passionate about environmental issues, collaboration, and global impact, this program offers a rare chance to learn, connect, and grow on an international stage. For more information visit here For more global opportunities click here The post Fully Funded EU4Ocean Summer School 2026 at the Mediterranean Sea and Black Sea appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: HCoC Youth Group Fellowship 2026–2027: Fully Funded Global Programme on Missile Non-Proliferation and Security Studies
summary: The HCoC Youth Group Fellowship 2026–2027 is a prestigious international programme designed for young professionals, graduate students, and early-career researchers interested in global security, arms control, and non-proliferation. Organized by the Fondation pour la Recherche Stratégique (FRS) with support from the European Union, this fellowship provides a unique platform to engage with leading experts, participate in high-level discussions, and contribute to policy-relevant research on ballistic missile proliferation. This second edition builds on the success of its inaugural cohort and responds to an increasingly complex global security environment characterized by rapid technological advancements, shifting geopolitical dynamics, and the growing spread of missile technologies. About the HCoC Youth Group Programme The HCoC Youth Group is part of a broader initiative supporting the Hague Code of Conduct against Ballistic Missile Proliferation (HCoC), an international effort aimed at promoting transparency, confidence-building, and restraint in missile development. The programme is structured as an 18-month fellowship that combines: Virtual learning and discussions In-person engagement with experts Independent research and publication Participants will join a global cohort of 15 fellows, creating a diverse and interdisciplinary learning environment. Programme Structure and Activities The HCoC Youth Group Fellowship offers a comprehensive and interactive learning experience. 1. Expert Webinars Fellows will participate in four thematic webinars scheduled across the programme timeline: November 2026 February 2027 April 2027 June 2027 Each session includes: Expert presentations by researchers, diplomats, and practitioners Moderated discussions Pre-submitted questions from fellows These sessions provide deep insights into missile non-proliferation and related global security issues. 2. Virtual Cafés Optional informal sessions will be held throughout the programme to: Foster peer interaction and collaboration Discuss current global developments Share research progress and ideas These sessions promote a collaborative and supportive learning environment. 3. In-Person Workshop (Lithuania) A key highlight of the programme is a three-day residential workshop scheduled for September 2027 in Plokštinė, Lithuania (subject to confirmation). Key features include: Expert briefings and discussions Research exchange sessions Simulation exercises (e.g., negotiation scenarios or war games) Guided visit to a former Cold War missile base All travel and accommodation costs for this workshop are fully covered by the programme. 4. Individual Research and Publication Each fellow is required to produce an individual research paper as part of the programme. Key aspects include: Selection of a research topic aligned with programme themes Development of an analytical paper Contribution to a collective publication edited by FRS This publication serves as a key output of the programme and enhances participants’ academic and professional profiles. Thematic Pillars The programme is structured around two core thematic pillars: Pillar 1: Technical Dimension Focus areas include: Emerging trends in missile proliferation Dual-use technologies and space systems Intersections between civilian and military technologies Implications for arms control frameworks Pillar 2: Diplomatic Dimension Focus areas include: Evaluation of the Hague Code of Conduct International cooperation and policy responses Challenges in a multipolar geopolitical landscape Recommendations for strengthening global non-proliferation efforts Applicants are required to indicate their preferred thematic focus during the application process. Eligibility Criteria The programme is open to a diverse group of candidates with relevant academic or professional backgrounds. Who Should Apply Young professionals Graduate students (Master’s level or above) Early-career researchers Required Qualifications Demonstrated interest in one or more of the following areas: Ballistic missile technology Arms control and strategic studies WMD non-proliferation Space policy and governance International law and diplomacy Regional security studies Full working proficiency in English Availability to participate in all programme activities Age requirement: Applicants must be under 40 years old Selection Criteria The selection committee will evaluate candidates based on: Quality and clarity of application Relevance of academic or professional background Geographic diversity Gender balance Interdisciplinary representation Time Commitment The programme is designed to be flexible while maintaining high engagement. Estimated commitment: 4–6 hours per month Additional time required for research and workshop preparation Why You Should Apply This fellowship provides a rare opportunity to engage with global security issues at a high level while developing research, analytical, and policy skills. It is particularly valuable for individuals aiming to build careers in international relations, defense policy, diplomacy, or strategic studies. Application Process Applicants must submit: Required Materials Completed online application form Curriculum Vitae (maximum 2 pages) Application Questions Include Motivation for joining the programme Key challenges in missile proliferation Contribution to the group Thematic interest A key question for experts All submissions must be in English. Application Deadline The deadline to apply for the HCoC Youth Group Fellowship 2026–2027 is: July 31, 2026 Applicants will be notified of the outcome within four weeks after the deadline. Find the Call for Applications document here . Applications must be submitted through the online form available here. Programme Benefits Participation in the programme offers numerous benefits, including: Free participation (no programme fees) Fully funded travel and accommodation for the in-person workshop Access to leading experts and policymakers Opportunity to publish research work Global networking and collaboration Practical experience in international security and diplomacy Terms and Conditions Fellows participate in a personal capacity All research outputs will be published under participants’ names Active participation throughout the programme is mandatory Non-participation may lead to removal from the programme Contact Information For inquiries, applicants can contact: m.garcia-carrasco@frstrategie.org Learn more about the HCoC Youth Group Fellowship HERE. Discover more global Fellowship and Research opportunities on OFY.
